title: 78K Tech Layoffs Show Who Gets AI and Who Doesn't
metaDescription: 78K layoffs in Q1 but it's not what you think. Companies are just picking sides between people who use AI and people who don't.
author: Marty @ CertFlo

sections.heading: The Numbers Are Pretty Clear
sections.body: New report from Nikkei Asia shows 78,557 tech layoffs in Q1 2026. US got hit with 76.7% of them. Here's the thing though - nearly half were because of AI and automation stuff. The data from Techmeme.com just confirms what most people already knew was happening. AI isn't coming to change how we work. It already did. But the headlines are missing the point. This isn't some crisis. It's the biggest sorting hat we've seen in decades. Companies are just picking between people who get AI and people who don't. Pretty obvious who's winning.

sections.heading: This Is Actually Good News If You're Paying Attention
sections.body:
Every big shift splits people into two groups. The ones who adapt early and the ones who don't. We're watching it happen right now with AI.

The people who saw this coming have been building their AI stuff for months already. They're getting promoted, running new projects, becoming the people their companies can't lose. While everyone else is worried about job security, these people are designing what comes next.

Think about it - if half the layoffs were AI-related, that means companies are actively choosing AI people over everyone else. They're not just cutting random jobs. They're making strategic moves about who can actually deliver when AI is running half the show.

sections.heading: The Gap Gets Bigger Every Day
sections.body: Here's what I've noticed covering this stuff - the early people don't just survive the change. They end up running it. Right now, while people are still debating whether AI will hit their industry, the smart ones are already learning ChatGPT, Claude, automation tools. They're building systems that make them 10x faster. Solving problems that used to need whole teams. Every day they wait, the gap gets wider. Every AI thing they figure out becomes a multiplier. The future belongs to people who can mix human thinking with AI automation, and honestly it's not as hard as people make it out to be - guides for beginners can get anyone up to speed pretty quick.

sections.heading: So What Are You Gonna Do
sections.body:
The data doesn't lie. Companies are betting big on AI automation and hiring accordingly. Question isn't whether this keeps going - it's whether you'll be ready when it speeds up.

The people building AI skills right now aren't just protecting their jobs. They're positioning themselves as the ones who'll define how work gets done for the next decade.
